title: AST SpaceMobile Provides Business Update and First Quarter 2026 Results
text: AST SpaceMobile announced Q1 2026 results and reaffirmed full-year 2026 guidance. The company reported Q1 revenue of $14.7 million versus analyst expectations around $36.58 million. The significant miss was accompanied by a loss per share of $0.66, well below the consensus estimate of $0.21. Operating expenses reached $164.1 million, driven by engineering costs of $84.1 million and general administrative costs of $43.7 million. The company carries $3.0 billion in long-term debt, with an anticipated Q2 2026 asset write-off of $155–160 million related to the BlueBird 7 satellite loss. Remaining performance obligations total $1.2 billion with contract liabilities of $233.0 million, indicating meaningful contracted demand. The company reaffirmed 2026 revenue guidance of $150-200 million, driven by mobile network partners and government contracts. The company maintains a strong cash position with $3.5 billion in liquidity to fund operations through profitability.
